title: After years of illness, Natick woman runs Boston Marathon with BAA
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Mackenzie Roy, a 32-year-old from Natick, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after years of battling autoimmune diseases.
Roy participates in the Marathon through B.A.A. Gives Back, promoting community, health, and fitness.
The Boston Marathon has been a long-time family tradition for Roy, inspiring her to join the race.

title: Bruins’ top prospect James Hagens welcomes challenge as he begins pro career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, begins pro hockey career with Providence Bruins after concluding Boston College sophomore season.
Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement and will debut against Springfield Thunderbirds on March 25.
Hagens, a top Bruins prospect, aims to earn a spot in Boston’s lineup amid their playoff push.

title: What Joe Mazzulla and Derrick White said about their Monday Night Raw appearance
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics' coach Joe Mazzulla and player Derrick White attended WWE Monday Night Raw at TD Garden for quality time.
Mazzulla and White described their playful interactions at the event, stressing the importance of off-court relationship building.
Mazzulla, an MMA fan, appreciates learning from various arenas, paralleling experiences to coaching on and off the court.

title: Comella’s closes Melrose location after 13 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Comella's has closed its Melrose location after 13 years due to operational and financial challenges.
The Melrose Comella's was independently owned and located at 478A Main St.
Comella's continues to operate 10 locations across Massachusetts, including Belmont, Concord, and Natick.

title: The most underrated movies of 2025 are streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Naked Gun' features Liam Neeson and Pamela Anderson in a comedic script by Paramount Pictures.
'The Ballad of Wallis Island' stars Tim Key and Carey Mulligan in a charming comedy-drama on Prime Video.
'28 Years Later' by Danny Boyle, a sequel to '28 Days Later', explores deeper themes on Netflix.

title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 22-year-old man was fatally shot by Westminster police during a confrontation in his home on South Ashburnham Road.
The man allegedly threatened officers with a large knife as they served an arrest warrant, prompting police to open fire.
Officers involved are on administrative leave; State Police and Westminster police are investigating the shooting.

title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Dratch went viral with her reaction to a 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' clue about the Spanish Armada.
Dratch learned about the Spanish Armada from her hairdresser Eddie Cook right before the show taping.
She discussed the serendipitous moment during an appearance on 'Late Night with Seth Meyers.'

title: She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Andrea Calleja, 46, will finish the World Marathon Majors by running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Calleja overcame a ruptured brain aneurysm that left her unable to walk, now advocating for the Brain Aneurysm Foundation.
She previously completed marathons in New York, Berlin, Chicago, Tokyo, London, and Sydney since 2017.

title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 261 at Para Maria, a pop-up bar dedicated to women's sports, is open at The Envoy Hotel in Boston's Seaport.
The bar's name, 261, honors Kathrine Switzer, the first woman to officially complete the Boston Marathon in 1967.
A dollar from cocktails sold supports Dream Big!, aiding sports equipment for girls from low-income households.

title: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay over $224,000 for diverting nonprofit funds and fraudulent pandemic claims.
Cannon-Grant received six months home confinement, 100 hours community service, and four years probation for fraud.
Federal Judge Angel Kelley cited $181,000 in donations and fraudulent benefits in setting the $224,063 forfeiture.
